symptoms.symptom_name: Generalised pruritus refractory to antihistamines, without a primary rash

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Cholestatic pruritus is characteristically antihistamine-refractory because it is not histamine-mediated; a rash-free generalised itch points away from a primary dermatosis.
Complete failure of antihistamines and absence of a rash argue against a histamine-driven allergic/dermatological cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Nocturnal worsening of itch with severe sleep deprivation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Cholestatic pruritus is classically worse at night; the resulting sleep deprivation defines the acute symptom crisis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Profound, disproportionate chronic fatigue for months

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fatigue is the commonest and often earliest symptom of PBC and is disproportionate to other findings.

symptoms.symptom_name: Sicca symptoms (dry eyes and dry mouth)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sjogren-type sicca overlap is common in PBC and adds specificity to the autoimmune cholestatic picture.
Xanthelasma reflects the hypercholesterolaemia of chronic cholestasis and is a recognised PBC stigma.
Melanin hyperpigmentation is a recognised cutaneous feature of chronic cholestasis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of jaundice (scleral icterus) reported by patient

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absent jaundice lowers (but does not exclude) an obstructing lesion; PBC pruritus/fatigue commonly precede jaundice.

symptoms.symptom_name: No fever, rigors or sudden severe abdominal pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of the Charcot's-triad features (fever/rigors + RUQ pain + jaundice) argues strongly against an acute biliary sepsis emergency.
Mild RUQ discomfort fits hepatomegaly but is non-specific; the absence of colic argues against biliary colic.

symptoms.symptom_name: Smooth, non-tender hepatomegaly ~3 cm below the costal margin; Murphy's sign negative; no ascites or spider naevi

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Smooth hepatomegaly supports chronic liver involvement; sealed clinician finding — the AI cannot palpate and must infer only from history.

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without AMA the autoimmune cholestatic diagnosis cannot be confirmed and definitive therapy is delayed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: AMA positivity with cholestatic biochemistry is highly specific for PBC — but it is a hospital/pathology test, never available in real time via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Isolated / predominant elevated alkaline phosphatase (ALP) on LFTs

investigation_details.expected_result: Cholestatic pattern — predominant ALP elevation (anticipated, pending formal pathology)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The cholestatic pattern that distinguishes PBC from a hepatitic process (autoimmune hepatitis) is missed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A predominant ALP rise defines the cholestatic biochemistry of PBC; requires a venous blood draw unavailable via telehealth.
AIH is transaminase-dominant; an ALP-predominant pattern argues against it.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: A middle-aged woman with antihistamine-refractory itch, disproportionate fatigue, sicca symptoms, xanthelasma and hyperpigmentation is the classic pre-icteric PBC picture.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Liver function tests including alkaline phosphatase (ALP) and bilirubin
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Establishes the cholestatic pattern and screens for obstruction/hepatocellular injury; the pivotal first blood test.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Anti-mitochondrial antibody (AMA) and autoimmune liver serology
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Highly specific for PBC; together with an ALP-predominant LFT pattern it confirms the diagnosis.
Chronic cholestasis causes hyperlipidaemia (explaining xanthelasma); useful baseline.
Excludes extrahepatic biliary obstruction (gallstone/stricture) as the not-to-miss mimic of cholestasis.
Cholestatic pruritus is not histamine-mediated; antihistamines have already wholly failed and escalating them wastes time and delays correct care.
